The virus HKU5-CoV-2 is a newly identified bat coronavirus that was described in a February 2025 Cell paper by Wuhan Institute of Virology and other institutions. While it has the pandemic-relevant ability to bind human ACE2 receptors in laboratory conditions, experts and public health organizations like the CDC have stated it does not pose a current public health concern. Up to the knowledge cutoff in mid-May 2025, zero human cases have been recorded. Newly discovered bat viruses almost never jump to humans and get sequenced/documented as a specific novel viral infection within months of their discovery. With only 1.5 months remaining until the July 1, 2025 resolution date, the probability of a natural spillover, or laboratory accident, causing a formally recognized human case in this specific short time window is virtually zero. I am estimating at the minimum allowable boundary (2%).
